instruction: Head across the room towards the front door then turn left. Go up the stairs and turn right. Enter the bedroom then wait.

system_prompt:
You are controlling a robot in a real indoor environment (a photorealistic 3D scan of a building). You interact only through these tools:

- observe(): look through the robot's forward-facing camera (RGB image plus a clearance readout: meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view; 10.0 = open).
- step(actions): execute movement actions in order. 0 = STOP (permanently ends the episode — declares you have reached the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.
- look_around(): one call returning four labeled views (ahead / right / behind / left); rotates 360 degrees and restores your heading (costs 24 turn steps).

Your task is to follow this navigation instruction to its endpoint:

"Head across the room towards the front door then turn left. Go up the stairs and turn right. Enter the bedroom then wait. "

Rules:
- Alternate observing and stepping: look, decide where the instruction wants you to go next, move, look again.
- You have a budget of 500 movement actions; each step() result reports roughly how many remain.
- You succeed only if you issue action 0 (STOP) while within 3 meters of the instruction's endpoint. STOP is permanent — issue it only when you believe you are at the goal.
- Turning in place (e.g. step([2,2,2,2,2,2])) is a cheap way to look around when unsure.
- Work autonomously until you stop; nobody can answer questions.
first_prompt: Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.

tool_schemas.name: observe
tool_schemas.description:
Look through the robot's forward-facing camera.

Returns the current egocentric RGB view plus a clearance readout: distance in meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view (10.0 = open, 10 m or more). If the goal object is centered ahead, clearance "center" is your true distance to it. Pure read — does not advance the simulator or consume step budget.

tool_schemas.name: look_around
tool_schemas.description:
Scan the surroundings in ONE call: rotates a full 360 degrees and
    returns four labeled views — ahead (0°), right (+90°), behind (+180°),
    left (+270°) — then restores the original heading exactly.

    Costs 24 low-level turn steps from the step budget but only one tool
    call. Use this at junctions and decision points instead of turning 15
    degrees at a time; your position and final heading are unchanged.

tool_schemas.name: step
tool_schemas.description:
Execute a sequence of movement actions, in order.

Actions: 0 = STOP (permanently ENDS the episode — issue it only when you believe the robot is within 3 meters of the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.

Executes sequentially and halts early if the episode ends. Returns how many actions ran, total steps taken, remaining budget, and whether the episode is over. The camera view changes after stepping — call observe() to see the result. Note: when plenty of budget remains, your FIRST STOP request is withheld pending a placement check — call step([0]) again to confirm and execute it.

text: Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.
I'll start by observing my surroundings.
I see a room with a fireplace, chairs, and windows. I need to find the front door. Let me scan around to get a full picture.

Let me analyze. Behind me is a kitchen with a window. To the right is a dining area leading to another room. To the left is a living room with a couch. Ahead is the fireplace room.

The front door is typically an exterior entrance. I need to "head across the room towards the front door." The left view (living room) opens up more space. Let me look more carefully. The right view shows a doorway leading to another area. Let me explore the left direction which has open space (clearance center 3.6).

Let me turn left to face that living room area and move across it.

I'm now facing the living room. There's a couch, coffee table, and windows. I need to find the front door. This looks like a living room but no obvious front door here. Let me move forward into this room to explore, since there may be an entrance area beyond.
Now I can see more clearly. There's a set of double doors on the left side — that looks like it could be the front door! Let me head towards it. I'll turn left slightly to face the doors.

Now I can see clearly. There's a staircase on the left, and the front door (double doors with glass panels) is in the center/right. The instruction says: "Head across the room towards the front door then turn left. Go up the stairs and turn right."

So I need to go towards the front door, then turn left to go up the stairs. The stairs are on the left. Let me move toward the front door area first.

I'm getting close to the front door area. The staircase is on my left. The instruction is to head to the front door, then turn left and go up the stairs. Let me move a bit closer to the door and then turn to face the stairs. Let me advance slightly.

The view didn't change much - there may be furniture blocking. The front door is in the center. Now I should turn left to face the stairs. The staircase is on the left side. Let me turn left to face it.
Very close to an obstacle now. Let me observe.
I turned into a wall/pillar. I turned too far. Let me turn back right to reorient and find the stairs.

I can see the staircase now on the right side of the view. There's a wall/pillar close on my left. I need to reposition. The stairs go up to the right. Let me back up a bit and reorient. Actually, I'm too close to the wall. Let me turn right more to face the stairs directly.

Now I can see the staircase ahead going up. There's a couch/chair in the foreground. The center is clearer now (2.2m). The stairs go up on the left/center. I need to move toward the base of the stairs. Let me back up first to get a better position — actually there's furniture in the foreground. Let me move forward carefully toward the stairs.

The view isn't changing — I might be stuck against furniture. The clearance is same. There's a chair/couch blocking. Let me turn right a bit to go around the furniture and approach the stairs from the more open right side.

Now I have more open space. I can see the front door area in the center-right and the staircase on the left. The stairs' base appears to be on the left. Let me navigate to the base of the stairs. The stairs go up starting from the left. Let me turn left to face the stairs and approach their base.

I'm back at the same spot facing the stairs but there's furniture (a couch) between me and the stairs. The staircase itself is at center-left. I need to get around this couch. The base of the stairs seems to be forward-left. Let me look around to understand the layout better around the stairs.

The stairs appear to ascend from the left. There's a light-colored couch directly in front of me blocking. Let me try moving forward towards the center where clearance is 2.2m and see if I can reach the stair base.

Clearance unchanged again - I'm not moving, blocked by furniture. The couch is in my path. Let me reposition. Let me back up and go around. First turn right and move to open space, then approach the stairs from a different angle.

Now I clearly see the front double doors (front door) on the left-center. This is the entryway. The stairs are to the left. According to instruction: head to front door, then turn left, go up stairs. 

I'm now near the front door. Let me turn left to look for the stairs.

Now I can see the staircase clearly on the left, and the front door on the right. The stairs ascend from left. There's a couch in the foreground. The base of the stairs is to the left. Let me turn left a bit more to face the stairs directly.

I see the staircase railings clearly now. There's a couch in front of me (foreground with orange pillow). The stairs go up behind the couch. I need to get to the base of the stairs which appears to be to the right side of this view. Let me turn right slightly and move toward where the stairs begin.

Now I can see the layout better. The front door is on the right. The staircase is on the left ascending upward. There's open floor space ahead (center 3.7m). The base of the stairs appears to be to my left. Let me move forward into this open area to reach the front door area, then I'll turn to the stairs.

Actually, I'm now in the entry hallway. The stairs go up on the left. Let me move forward a bit to the open landing near the front door, then turn left toward the stairs base.

Now I'm in the entry foyer. The front door is directly ahead-right (double doors with glass). The staircase is on the left. I've reached the front door area. Now per instruction: "turn left. Go up the stairs." The stairs are behind me on the left. Let me turn left to face the staircase.
Now I'm facing the staircase directly. I can see the stairs going up. Let me move toward the base of the stairs and start climbing. Let me turn slightly to center on the stairs.
The stairs are on the right side now. There's a couch in the foreground. The stairs ascend to the right. Let me turn right to face the base of the stairs.

Now I'm facing the staircase directly - the stairs ascend right in front of me. The clearance is low (0.4m) which means the first step is right there. Let me climb the stairs by moving forward. The stairs go up and to the right (they curve). Let me move forward.
I'm progressing up the stairs. The staircase continues up and appears to curve. I can see the upper level. There seems to be a landing. The stairs continue up-left. Let me continue climbing.

I appear to be near a landing. The stairs continue up on the left, and there's an open area/hallway on the right. There's decorative greenery on the railing. The clearance on the right is more open (2.7m). The stairs seem to continue up. Let me look more carefully - there's a landing and the stairs may continue. Let me turn to see the full landing area.

I can see now there's a hallway ahead (center 3.5m open) leading to what looks like the upper floor with doorways. This appears to be the top of the stairs / a landing on the upper level. There's an open hallway ahead with doors. 

But wait, I need to check if the stairs continue up. The staircase on the left seems to continue upward. Let me look around to understand the full layout up here.

Now I understand the layout. To my left (+270°) the stairs continue up — there's more staircase going up! So I'm on a landing partway up. The instruction says "Go up the stairs and turn right. Enter the bedroom."

The stairs continue to my left. Let me continue up the stairs first. Let me turn left to face the continuing staircase.
Now I'm facing the continuing staircase. It curves up. Let me climb these stairs. They turn upward to the right. Let me move forward.
The stairs curve up to the right. I'm at the turn. The stairs continue up on the right side. Let me turn right to follow the staircase up.
The stairs continue up ahead. I can see the upper landing/floor at the top right. Let me continue climbing.
I'm facing a wall now. The stairs continue to the right where there's more visible. Let me turn right to follow the stairs up to the top.
I can see the top of the stairs now — there's a landing area ahead-right with wood flooring. The stairs continue up on the right. Let me turn right a bit more and continue up.

I've reached the top of the stairs — an upper landing/hallway with wood floors, a mirror, and a console table. Now per instruction: "Go up the stairs and turn right. Enter the bedroom then wait."

I'm now at the top. I need to turn right and find the bedroom. Let me look around to see the layout up here and find bedroom doorways.

Now I'm at the top of the stairs. Let me analyze:
- Ahead (0°): console table, mirror, closed area
- Right (+90°): hallway with railing (overlooking stairs), leads to another area with a room
- Behind (+180°): wall with decorations, and stairs area behind
- Left (+270°): a doorway with double doors (closet?) and an open doorway on the right leading to a room

The instruction says "turn right. Enter the bedroom." I came up the stairs facing this direction. When I finished the stairs, I turned. The "turn right" after the stairs — the right view shows a hallway leading to what appears to be another room/bedroom area.

Let me examine the right direction more. It leads down a hallway. Let me turn right and go that way.

Now I'm facing the hallway. On the left there's an open room (leads to a room with windows - possibly a bedroom), the center has a railing overlooking the stairwell, and the right has the balustrade. The hallway leads forward toward a room in the distance.

Let me consider: the instruction says "enter the bedroom." There's a room ahead-left through the doorway. Let me head down the hallway. The right side is more open (3.7m). Let me move forward along the hallway toward the room ahead.

I turned too much toward the railing/stairwell. I see the balcony railing overlooking the living room below. Let me reorient. The room I want is to the left along the hallway. Let me turn left to get back toward the hallway/room.

I'm near the console table and hallway. There's a room ahead through a doorway (the ceiling light area). Let me reposition. There's the railing to my left. The open room is ahead/center. Let me back up and reorient. Let me look at the layout — I seem to be quite close to obstacles. Let me turn right to face the open hallway/room.

I see. There's a doorway/opening on the left leading to a room (with ceiling light and windows). This looks like it could be the bedroom. The right side is the balcony overlooking the living room. 

Let me reconsider the whole route. I came up the stairs. The instruction: "Go up the stairs and turn right. Enter the bedroom." 

Looking at earlier look_around at top of stairs: the "left" (+270°) view had a doorway leading into a room with windows. Actually that might be the bedroom. But the instruction says turn right.

The room to my left now (with the ceiling light chandelier) is accessible. Let me head into that room. Let me turn left to face it and enter.

I see the console table with the mirror in front of me. There's an opening to a room on the right side (with windows and ceiling light). The console table is blocking my center. This is a hallway/landing area. 

Let me re-examine. The room with the chandelier/windows to the right of the mirror appears to be a living space or bedroom. Let me navigate around the console table. Let me back up and go toward that room opening on the right.

Now I can clearly see a BEDROOM through the doorway on the left! I can see a bed with pillows. This is the bedroom the instruction refers to. Let me head toward that doorway and enter the bedroom. It's on the left. Let me turn left to face the bedroom doorway.

I can see the bedroom through the doorway ahead-right, with the bed visible. But there's the console table in front of me (center 0.5m blocked). The bedroom doorway is to the right of the mirror. Let me navigate around the table toward the bedroom door. Let me turn right and move toward the doorway.

The bedroom doorway is now more centered/left. I can see the bed clearly. The center clearance is only 0.6 (there's the console table edge). Let me move slightly left toward the doorway. Let me turn left a touch and approach.

Stop exploring new areas; commit to your best goal candidate, approach it, and STOP before the budget runs out."}

text: The center is now open (2.3m) toward the bedroom doorway. I can see the bed clearly through the opening. Let me move forward into the bedroom. The doorway is straight ahead now.
